title: Functions
description: Explore declaration, arguments, return values, overloading, and performance optimizations like inline functions in C++.
learningObjectives: Declare and define functions with parameters and return values
Contrast function overloading with unique parameter types
Use inline functions to avoid stack frame overhead in math helper code

sections.title: Reusable Logic and Calling Conventions
sections.content: Functions group statements into modular, callable blocks of code. When a function is called, the CPU pushes local variables and return registers to the stack. For tiny, high-frequency helper functions (like vector additions or distance calculations), this stack frame creation introduces performance overhead. Declaring these helpers `inline` signals the compiler to copy the function body directly to the call site, eliminating call overhead.

sections.title: Inline Helpers and Overloading
